sections.label: Section 1132
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἶμι , παιδὸς ἀριθμὸν ὡς τελεσφόρον
sections.translation: But I will go to make the tenth-day sacrifice to the gods for the child ; and when I have done you this favor , I will go to the field where my husband is sacrificing to the

sections.label: Section 1135
sections.translation: Nymphs . Take this team away , my attendants , and bring it to the stalls ; and when you think that I have finished this sacrifice to the gods , be ready ; for I must also please my husband .

sections.label: Section 1139
sections.original: χώρει πένητας ἐς δόμους · φρούρει δέ μοι
sections.translation: Go into a poor house ; but please take care

sections.label: Section 1140
sections.original: μή σʼ αἰθαλώσῃ πολύκαπνον στέγος πέπλους .
sections.translation: that my smoke-grimed walls do not smear your robes with soot . For you will make the sacrifice to the gods that you ought to make . Going in to the house . The basket is ready , and the knife sharpened , the same that killed the bull by whose side you will lie , struck down . Even in Hades ’ house you will be the bride of the one

sections.label: Section 1145
sections.original: ᾧπερ ξυνηῦδες ἐν φάει . τοσήνδʼ ἐγὼ
sections.translation: whom you slept with in life . This is the favor I will give you , and you will give me retribution for my father . Exit Electra .

sections.label: Section 1147
sections.translation: Requital for evils ; the breezes of the house shift and blow . At another time my leader , my own , fell murdered in the bath ,

sections.label: Section 1150
sections.original: ἰάχησε δὲ στέγα λάινοί τε θριγκοὶ δόμων ,
sections.translation: and the roof and stone walls of the house cried aloud , while he said : O cruelty ! My wife , why are you murdering me on my return to my dear country in the tenth year ? . . .

sections.label: Section 1155
sections.translation: Retribution for straying love has flowed back and brings to judgment the one who killed her wretched husband , when he came at last to his home and to the towering Cyclopean walls ; with her own hand she killed him with the sharp-edged weapon ,

sections.label: Section 1160
sections.original: ἔκανεν αὐτόχειρ , πέλεκυν ἐν χεροῖν λαβοῦσʼ · ἆ τλάμων
sections.translation: holding the axe in her hands . Unhappy husband ! whatever the curse that possessed that wretched woman . Like a lioness of the hills that ranges through the meadowland woods , she accomplished these things .

sections.label: Section 1165
sections.original: ὦ τέκνα , πρὸς θεῶν , μὴ κτάνητε μητέρα .
sections.translation: O children , by the gods , do not kill your mother .

sections.label: Section 1166
sections.translation: Do you hear her cries within the house ?

sections.label: Section 1167
sections.translation: O God ! Ah me !

sections.label: Section 1168
sections.translation: I also wail for you , overpowered by your children . Truly the god deals out justice , whenever it befalls .

sections.label: Section 1170
sections.original: σχέτλια μὲν ἔπαθες , ἀνόσια δʼ εἰργάσω ,
sections.translation: You have suffered cruelly , unhappy one , yet you did unholy things to your husband .

sections.label: Section 1172
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οἵδε μητρὸς νεοφόνοις ἐν αἵμασι
sections.translation: But here they come from the house , defiled in the newly shed blood of their mother , a triumphal rout , evidence of the pitiable sacrifice .

sections.label: Section 1175
sections.translation: There is no house more pitiable than the race of Tantalus , nor has there ever been .

sections.label: Section 1.1177
sections.translation: O Earth , and Zeus who sees all mortal acts , look at these loathsome bloody deeds , these two bodies

sections.label: Section 1.1180
sections.translation: lying on the earth at the blow from my hand , atonement for my suffering . . .

sections.label: Section 1.1182
sections.original: δακρύτʼ ἄγαν , ὦ σύγγονʼ , αἰτία δʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: Too many tears , my brother , and I am the cause . Unhappy , that I came to fiery rage against this woman , who was my mother !

sections.label: Section 1.1185
sections.translation: Alas for your fate ; you gave birth to unbearable pain , and you suffered it , miserably and beyond , from your children . Yet you have rightly paid for their father ’ s murder .

sections.label: Section 1.1190
sections.translation: Ah , Phoebus ! you proclaimed in song unclear justice , but you have brought about clear woes , and granted me a bloody destiny far from the land of Hellas . To what other city can I go ?

sections.label: Section 1.1195
sections.translation: What host , what pious man will look at me , who killed my mother ?

sections.label: Section 1.1198
sections.original: ἰὼ ἰώ μοι . ποῖ δʼ ἐγώ , τίνʼ ἐς χορόν ,
sections.translation: Ah me ! Where can I go , to what dance , to what marriage ? What husband will receive me

sections.label: Section 1.1200
sections.translation: into the bridal bed ?

sections.label: Section 1.1201
sections.translation: Again , again your thought changes with the breeze ; for now you think piously , though you did not before , and you did dreadful things ,

sections.label: Section 1.1205
sections.translation: my dear , to your unwilling brother .

sections.label: Section 2.1206
sections.original: κατεῖδες , οἷον ἁ τάλαινʼ ἔξω πέπλων
sections.translation: Did you see how the unhappy one threw off her robe and showed her bosom in the slaughter , alas , hurling to the ground the limbs that gave me birth ? And her hair , I —

sections.label: Section 2.1210
sections.translation: I know it well ; you passed through agony , hearing the mournful wail of the mother that bore you .

sections.label: Section 2.1214
sections.original: βοὰν δʼ ἔλασκε τάνδε , πρὸς γένυν ἐμὰν
sections.translation: She uttered this cry , putting her hand to my chin :

sections.label: Section 2.1215
sections.translation: My child , I entreat you ! And she clung to my cheeks , so that the sword fell from my hand .

sections.label: Section 2.1218
sections.translation: The unhappy one ! How did you endure to see the blood

sections.label: Section 2.1220
sections.translation: of your mother , breathing her last before your eyes ?

sections.label: Section 3.1221
sections.original: ἐγὼ μὲν ἐπιβαλὼν φάρη κόραις ἐμαῖς
sections.translation: I threw my cloak over my eyes , and began the sacrifice by plunging the sword into my mother ’ s throat .

sections.label: Section 3.1224
sections.translation: And I urged you on and

sections.label: Section 3.1225
sections.translation: put my hand to the sword together with you .

sections.label: Section 3.1226
sections.translation: You have done the most dreadful of deeds .

sections.label: Section 3.1227
sections.translation: Take and hide the limbs of our mother beneath a robe , and close the wounds . Turning to the corpse Ah ! You gave birth to your own murderers .

sections.label: Section 3.1230
sections.translation: There , I am putting this cloak over the one loved and not loved .

sections.label: Section 3.1232
sections.translation: An end of great troubles for the house .

sections.label: Section 1233
sections.translation: Divine forms are seen above the house . But see the ones who are appearing over the top of the house — spirits or gods from heaven ?

sections.label: Section 1235
sections.original: τῶν οὐρανίων ; οὐ γὰρ θνητῶν γʼ
sections.translation: For this path does not belong to men . Why ever do they come into the clear sight of mortals ?

sections.label: Section 1238
sections.original: Ἀγαμέμνονος παῖ , κλῦθι · δίπτυχοι δέ σε
sections.translation: Son of Agamemnon , listen ; the twin sons of Zeus , your mother ’ s brothers ,

sections.label: Section 1240
sections.translation: Castor and his brother Polydeuces , are calling you . Having just now calmed the swell of the sea , terrible for ships , we have come to Argos when we saw the slaying of our sister , your mother . Now she has her just reward , but you have not acted justly ,

sections.label: Section 1245
sections.original: Φοῖβός τε , Φοῖβος — ἀλλʼ ἄναξ γάρ ἐστʼ ἐμός ,
sections.translation: and Phoebus , Phoebus — but I am silent , for he is my lord ; although he is wise , he gave you oracles that were not . But it is necessary to accept these things . As to what remains , you must do what Fate and Zeus have accomplished for you . Give Electra to Pylades as his wife to take to his home ;

sections.label: Section 1250
sections.original: σὺ δʼ Ἄργος ἔκλιπʼ · οὐ γὰρ ἔστι σοι πόλιν
sections.translation: but you leave Argos ; for it is not for you , who killed your mother , to set foot in this city . And the dread goddesses of death , the one who glare like hounds , will drive you up and down , a maddened wanderer . Go to Athens and embrace the holy image of Pallas ;

sections.label: Section 1255
sections.translation: for she will prevent them , flickering with dreadful serpents , from touching you , as she stretches over your head her Gorgon-faced shield . There is a hill of Ares , where the gods first sat over their votes to decide on bloodshed ,

sections.label: Section 1260
sections.translation: when savage Ares killed Halirrothius , son of the ocean ’ s ruler , in anger for the unholy violation of his daughter , so that the tribunal is most sacred and secure in the eyes of the gods .

sections.label: Section 1264
sections.original: ἐνταῦθα καὶ σὲ δεῖ δραμεῖν φόνου πέρι .
sections.translation: You also must run your risk here , for murder .

sections.label: Section 1265
sections.original: ἴσαι δέ σʼ ἐκσῴζουσι μὴ θανεῖν δίκῃ
sections.translation: An equal number of votes cast will save you from dying by the verdict ; for Loxias will take the blame upon himself , since it was his oracle that advised your mother ’ s murder . And this law will be set for posterity , that the accused will always win his case if he has equal votes .

sections.label: Section 1270
sections.original: δειναὶ μὲν οὖν θεαὶ τῷδʼ ἄχει πεπληγμέναι
sections.translation: Then the dread goddesses , stricken with grief at this , will sink into a cleft of the earth beside this hill , a holy , revered prophetic shrine for mortals . You must found an Arcadian city beside the streams of Alpheus near the sacred enclosure to Lycaean Apollo ;

sections.label: Section 1275
sections.translation: and the city will be called after your name . I say this to you . As for this corpse of Aegisthus , the citizens of Argos will cover it in the earth in burial . But as for your mother , Menelaus , who has arrived at Nauplia only now after capturing Troy ,

sections.label: Section 1280
sections.original: Ἑλένη τε θάψει · Πρωτέως γὰρ ἐκ δόμων
sections.translation: will bury her , with Helen helping him ; for she has come from Proteus ’ house , leaving Egypt , and she never went to Troy ; Zeus , to stir up strife and bloodshed among mortals , sent a phantom of Helen to Ilium . Now let Pylades , having one who is both a virgin and a married woman ,

sections.label: Section 1285
sections.translation: go home from the Achaean land , and let him conduct the one called your brother-in-law to the land of Phocis , and give him a weight of riches . But you set out along the narrow Isthmus , and go to Cecropia ’ s blessed hill .

sections.label: Section 1290
sections.translation: For once you have completed your appointed lot of murder , you will be happy , freed from these troubles .

sections.label: Section 1292
sections.original: ὦ παῖδε Διός , θέμις ἐς φθογγὰς
sections.translation: Sons of Zeus , is it right for us to draw near to speak with you ?

sections.label: Section 1294
sections.translation: It is right , for those not polluted by this murder .

sections.label: Section 1295
sections.translation: May I too share your conversation , sons of Tyndareus ?

sections.label: Section 1296
sections.translation: You too ; to Phoebus I will attribute this bloody deed .

sections.label: Section 1298
sections.original: πῶς ὄντε θεὼ τῆσδέ τʼ ἀδελφὼ
sections.translation: How was it that you , being gods and the brothers of this murdered woman ,

sections.label: Section 1300
sections.translation: did not keep the death-goddesses away from her house ?
